name: Balendra Shah
biography: Balendra Shah (widely known as Balen Shah) is the president of the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) and the party's prime ministerial candidate for the 2026 general election. Born in Kathmandu on April 27, 1990, he trained as a civil engineer before rising to fame as one of Nepal's most prominent hip-hop artists, known for lyrics addressing corruption and inequality. He served as the 15th Mayor of Kathmandu from 2022, resigning in January 2026 to contest the general election. He defeated former four-time Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli by a historic margin of 49,614 votes in the Jhapa-5 constituency.

majorContributions.title: Kathmandu Urban Renewal

majorContributions.title: Youth Political Mobilization

name: Ranju Darshana
biography: Ranju Darshana Neupane, born in 1996, is a Nepali politician and activist who entered politics at age 17 with Bibeksheel Nepali Party, ran for Kathmandu mayor at 21 in 2017, and served as general secretary of Bibeksheel Sajha Party before joining Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP). She won the Kathmandu-1 parliamentary seat in the 2026 election (2082 BS), becoming RSP's first declared victor just 12 days after childbirth, defeating rivals by over 9,000 votes. Previously a lecturer at Thames International College and advocate for women's rights, RTI, and youth opportunities, she is known for her role in anti-corruption protests and social campaigns. [MyRepublica](https://myrepublica.nagariknetwork.com/news/rsps-ranju-darshana-wins-kathmandu-1-68-35.html), [Kathmandu Post](https://kathmandupost.com/kathmandu/2026/03/06/rsp-s-ranju-darshana-wins-kathmandu-1), [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranju_Darshana)

name: Surya Bahadur Lama
biography: Surya Bahadur Lama, aged 41, is a politician who won the House of Representatives seat from Udayapur-2 constituency for Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) in the 2026 Nepal general election, securing 14,802 votes against UML rival Ambar Bahadur Rayamajhi's 7,648. His highest education is +2 level, with no prior professional background detailed in available sources. This marks RSP's strong performance in the region, contributing to their landslide victory.

name: Bishwa Raj Pokharel
biography: Bishwa Raj Pokharel is a former Additional Inspector General (AIG) of Nepal Police with 29 years in police service, 5 years in judicial service, and 2 years in administrative service, having joined as an inspector in 1993 and resigned in 2022 after a controversial IGP promotion dispute. He joined the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) in November 2025 and won the Okhaldhunga-1 seat in the 2026 general election by a narrow margin of 5 votes. Born in Kathmandu and residing there, he holds an MA degree.

name: Som Sharma
biography: Som Sharma is a 34-year-old journalist, social activist, and entrepreneur from Baglung, Nepal, who worked as a computer technician, teacher, and managing director of an electronics firm while advocating for good governance through campaigns like 'Hamro Baglung Hamro Jimmewari' and participation in the Gen Z movement and Maitighar protests. He has contributed to local media via ebaglung.com and protested issues like Kaligandaki River exploitation and corruption. In the 2026 Nepal general election, he won the Baglung-2 seat for Rastriya Swatantra Party with 12,647 votes, defeating the Nepali Congress candidate.
